findings.evidence: `nl -ba TauCeti/KnotTheory/Grid/MarkingSwap.lean` shows `swapMarkings_swapMarkings` at lines 104-106 and `transpose_swapMarkings` at lines 109-111 both close with `rfl`; existing `GridDiagram.transpose_transpose` in `TauCeti/KnotTheory/Grid/Diagram.lean:809` uses `ext c <;> simp`.
findings.fix: Prove these with `ext c <;> simp [swapMarkings]` or equivalent extensional/simp proofs, matching the nearby `transpose_transpose` style, so later implementation changes do not break the API lemma accidentally.
findings.issue: `swapMarkings_swapMarkings` and especially `transpose_swapMarkings` prove operational diagram equalities by bare `rfl`, so they depend on the current record-constructor definitions of `swapMarkings`/`transpose` and proof-field erasure rather than the extensional marking content.

summary: The new projection and grading proofs are mostly direct uses of existing definitional APIs, but the higher-level symmetry proofs rely on undocumented definitional equality between composed diagram constructors.
